pattern: The AND of a range equals the common prefix of left and right in binary; right-shift both together until they match, then shift the result back.
hints: Bit k is 1 in the AND result only if every number in [left, right] has bit k set; whenever the range spans an even/odd pair that bit flips to 0.
Consecutive integers share a common binary prefix; once the values diverge, every lower bit will cycle through 0 and 1 within the range, making those bits contribute 0 to the AND.
Keep right-shifting left and right by one until they are equal — that common value is the shared prefix. Left-shift it back by the number of shifts taken to get the answer.

edge_inputs.description: single number in range — AND is the number itself
left is zero — AND is always 0
range of two consecutive integers that differ only in the last bit
range crosses a power of two — shared prefix shrinks dramatically
left=1, right=INT_MAX — all lower bits cycle, result is 0
both at maximum value — single-element range
small range where all numbers share a high-bit prefix
range that spans a block boundary losing the second-highest bit

stress.description: random left/right pairs of varying gap sizes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    left = rng.randint(0, 2**30)
    gap = rng.randint(0, min(size, 2**20))
    right = min(left + gap, 2147483647)
    return [left, right]
stress.note: covers both small and large gaps
